question.content: In the diode-circuit set, use panel (b) with diode drop UD=0.7 V to determine output voltage Uo2. Output exactly one decimal number with 2 decimal places, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: a decimal number rounded to two decimal places; write only the numerical value itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Set up piecewise-linear diode equations with unknown node voltages and branch currents.
step_1.key_point: Use complementarity constraints instead of sequential state guessing.

step_3.content: For each diode impose V_D>=0.7, I_D>=0, and (V_D-0.7)*I_D=0.
step_3.key_point: Only one active-set combination satisfies all inequalities simultaneously.

step_5.content: Solve the linear KCL/KVL system for that feasible active set and get candidate Uo2.
step_5.key_point: Output value comes directly from the solved node voltages.

step_7.content: Verify all inactive/active consistency inequalities using the candidate solution.
step_7.key_point: Consistency check confirms uniqueness of the operating state.

step_9.content: Format the final value to two decimals and output only the number.
